response.justification: Staff Software Engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level role creates an unusual mismatch. While technically exceptional across all required skills (TypeScript, React, Node.js, databases, REST APIs) with proven shipping ability at scale, the seniority gap raises significant concerns: likely flight risk, compensation expectations misalignment, and potential frustration with scope. Interview should clarify motivation—if genuinely pivoting to renewable energy or seeking hands-on work, this would be an excellent hire. Without that context, overqualification poses real retention risk.
strengths: Exceptional fullstack depth with 15+ years shipping production systems across backend, frontend, databases, and DevOps
Mastered all required technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, REST APIs) plus AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship (TDD, clean architecture) demonstrated through mentoring and teaching track record
concerns: Staff-level engineer applying for entry-level position suggests flight risk and likely frustration with scope after initial period
PostgreSQL not explicitly mentioned in skills; current role description vague on hands-on React and relational database work
Compensation expectations for this career level almost certainly exceed typical junior/mid-level offer, creating negotiation friction

key_factors.factor: Technical skills match across all requirements
